findings.evidence: Lines 49-63 use only `[Group G] [MulAction G X]` structure: the proof only applies `MulAction.orbitRel_apply`, `Subgroup.mem_bot`, and `one_smul`; Mathlib already has the natural target equivalence for a bottom setoid at `mathlib/Mathlib/Data/Setoid/Basic.lean:237`, `def quotientBotEquiv : Quotient (⊥ : Setoid α) ≃ α`.
findings.fix: First prove a general theorem such as `MulAction.orbitRel_bot_eq_bot` and/or `MulAction.orbitRelQuotientBotEquiv : MulAction.orbitRel.Quotient (⊥ : Subgroup G) X ≃ X`, then define the deck-fibre equivalence as its specialization.
findings.issue: `eq_of_orbitRel_bot` and `subgroupFiberOrbitQuotientBotEquiv` prove a generic fact only for `Deck p` acting on one fibre, creating an under-general public API.

summary: The bottom-orbit API is stated at a deck-fibre level even though the main result is a generic fact about any group action. Move the trivial-subgroup orbit quotient equivalence to the general `MulAction` level and derive the deck wrappers from it.
